title: Prefer
questions: Do you prefer studying English using books or Internet resources?
When do you prefer to study, in the morning, afternoon or evening?
Do your teachers ever ask you what you prefer doing?
Do you prefer to have a choice in class or do what your teacher thinks is best?
Do you prefer doing lessons on reading, writing, speaking or listening?
Would you prefer to have a different teacher?
How do you prefer to study?
Which do you prefer, studying in class with a teacher or studying alone?
What's the difference between 'prefer doing something' and 'prefer to do something'?
Would you prefer an English teacher from your own country or one from an English-speaking country?
Do you prefer paper dictionaries or online / electronic dictionaries?
What topics do you prefer to talk about in class and which do you prefer to not talk about?
Do you prefer talking with the person next to you in class or moving around and talking to different students?
Do you prefer male or female teachers?
Do you prefer your teacher explaining grammar on the board or working it out for yourself?
Have you ever told your teacher what you prefer doing in class?
What things do you think your teacher might prefer to do differently in class?
What are the differences between 'prefer' and 'would rather'?
Would you prefer to have more, less or no homework?
What would you prefer to do instead of having this discussion?
